message:
feat(observability+scheduler): Deadlocked health classification from explicit trapped wait-cycle evidence

The spectral health monitor previously could only infer structural
fragmentation from eigenvalue analysis, which is a topology signal
but not proof of actual deadlock.  When directional SCC analysis
has already identified a trapped wait cycle, that evidence should
be surfaced as a distinct Deadlocked classification.

Spectral health (spectral_health.rs):
- New HealthClassification::Deadlocked variant for caller-provided
  trapped-cycle evidence, distinct from the topology-inferred
  Fragmented.
- New analyze_with_trapped_cycle() method accepts a trapped_wait_cycle
  bool hint; when true, returns Deadlocked regardless of spectral
  analysis.  Existing analyze() delegates with false.
- Display, variant list in tests, and module docs updated.
- Test: monitor_trapped_cycle_hint_reports_deadlocked.

Diagnostics (diagnostics.rs):
- Extracted wait_graph_adjacency() helper (was inline in
  analyze_deadlocks).
- New has_trapped_wait_cycle() performs directed SCC analysis on the
  adjacency list and returns true if any SCC of size >= 2 exists.
- structural_health() now passes trapped-cycle evidence to the
  spectral monitor via analyze_with_trapped_cycle().
- Test: structural_health_reports_deadlocked_for_trapped_cycle
  constructs a minimal t1↔t2 cycle and verifies Deadlocked output.

Scheduler (three_lane.rs):
- Governor spectral path now calls analyze_with_trapped_cycle() with
  the already-computed trapped_wait_cycle flag, and runs the spectral
  monitor even for single-node graphs when a trapped cycle exists.
- New Deadlocked match arm maps to DrainObligations suggestion.
- Test: test_governor_single_live_task_without_wait_edges_skips_spectral_monitor
  verifies that a single task with no wait edges does not trigger
  spectral analysis or obligation draining.
- Test: trapped cycle with two tasks verifies DrainObligations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
